entries.title: Half-Angle Formulas
entries.content: Trigonometric identities that express trigonometric functions of half-angles in terms of functions of the original angles.
entries.example: Finding exact value of sin(15 degrees)

entries.title: Half-Life Exponential Decay
entries.content: The time required for a quantity to reduce to half of its initial value.
entries.example: Decay of radioactive Carbon-14 with half-life of 5730 years

entries.title: Hamilton's Quaternions
entries.content: A four-dimensional number system extending complex numbers, useful for 3D rotations.
entries.example: Calculating rotation in computer graphics and game engines

entries.title: Hamiltonian Cycle
entries.content: A closed loop path in a graph that visits each vertex exactly once.
entries.example: Solving the traveling salesperson problem path

entries.title: Hankel Matrix
entries.content: A square matrix with constant skew-diagonals.
entries.example: Used in signal processing and time series analysis

entries.title: Harmonic Mean
entries.content: The reciprocal of the arithmetic mean of the reciprocals of a set of values.
entries.example: Calculating average speed for round trip of different speeds

entries.title: Harmonic Progression
entries.content: A sequence of numbers whose reciprocals form an arithmetic progression.

entries.title: Harmonic Series
entries.content: The divergent infinite series of the reciprocals of the positive integers.

entries.title: Hausdorff Space
entries.content: A topological space in which any two distinct points have disjoint neighborhoods.
entries.formula: For x != y, exists U, V with x in U, y in V, U intersect V = empty
entries.example: Most standard spaces like Euclidean space are Hausdorff

entries.title: Heaviside Step Function
entries.content: A step function that is zero for negative arguments and one for positive arguments.
entries.example: Representing sudden switch turning on in circuit modeling

entries.title: Heptagon Area
entries.content: The area of a regular seven-sided polygon of side length s.
entries.example: Regular heptagon with side length 5

entries.title: Hermitian Matrix
entries.content: A square matrix that is equal to its own conjugate transpose.
entries.example: Hermitian matrices have real eigenvalues

entries.title: Heron's Formula
entries.content: A formula for the area of a triangle given the lengths of all three sides.
entries.example: Finding area of triangle with sides 5, 12, 13

entries.title: Heron's Area Calculation
entries.content: Using Heron's formula to determine triangle area.
entries.example: Sides 3, 4, 5 -> semiperimeter s=6 -> Area=6

entries.title: Hexadecimal Conversion
entries.content: Converting numbers between base-10 decimal and base-16 hexadecimal systems.
entries.example: Hex colors in web CSS like #FFFFFF

entries.title: Hexagon Area
entries.content: The area of a regular six-sided polygon of side length s.
entries.example: Area of regular hexagon with side length 4 is 24*sqrt(3)

entries.title: Hilbert Space
entries.content: An inner product space that is also a complete metric space.
entries.formula: V with inner product and complete norm
entries.example: Quantum mechanics state spaces are Hilbert spaces

entries.title: Histogram Representation
entries.content: A graphical representation of the distribution of numerical data by grouping into bins.
entries.formula: Heights of bars show frequencies in intervals
entries.example: Plotting age distribution of survey respondents

entries.title: Homeomorphism
entries.content: A continuous bijection between topological spaces with a continuous inverse.
entries.formula: f: X -> Y is continuous, bijective, f^-1 is continuous
entries.example: A coffee mug is homeomorphically equivalent to a donut

entries.title: Homogeneous Coordinates
entries.content: A system of coordinates used in projective geometry, representing N-dimensional points with N+1 coordinates.
entries.example: Graphics transformations using 4x4 matrices for 3D points

entries.title: Homogeneous Linear ODE
entries.content: A linear ordinary differential equation in which the constant term is zero.
entries.example: Solving y'' + 4y = 0

entries.title: Homomorphism
entries.content: A structure-preserving map between two algebraic structures of the same type.
entries.example: Mapping vectors through a linear transformation matrix

entries.title: Hooke's Law Formula
entries.content: Physical formula relating spring force to displacement, representing linear variation.
entries.example: Force exerted by a stretched spring

entries.title: Horizontal Asymptote
entries.content: A horizontal line that the graph of a function approaches as x goes to positive or negative infinity.
entries.formula: y = L where lim x->inf f(x) = L
entries.example: Horizontal asymptote of y = (2x+1)/(x-3) is y = 2

entries.title: Horizontal Line Test
entries.content: A test used to determine if a function is one-to-one (injective) and has a valid inverse function.
entries.formula: If any horizontal line crosses the graph >1 time, not 1-to-1
entries.example: f(x) = x^2 fails, f(x) = x^3 passes

entries.title: Hyperbola Equation
entries.content: The algebraic equation representing a curve with two open branches.
entries.example: Hyperbola with asymptotes y = +- (b/a)x

entries.title: Hyperbolic Cosine
entries.content: A hyperbolic function defined using exponential functions.
entries.example: Shape of a hanging cable (catenary curve)

entries.title: Hyperbolic Sine
entries.content: A hyperbolic function defined using exponential functions.
entries.example: Solving heat and wave differential equations

entries.title: Hyperbolic Tangent
entries.content: A hyperbolic function representing the ratio of sinh to cosh, used as an activation function.
entries.example: Neural network activation scaling output to (-1, 1)

entries.title: Hypergeometric Distribution
entries.content: A discrete probability distribution of drawing successes from a finite population without replacement.
entries.example: Probability of drawing 3 red marbles out of 10 without replacement

entries.title: Hypergraph
entries.content: A generalization of a graph in which an edge can join any number of vertices.
entries.formula: H = (X, E) where edges in E are subsets of X
entries.example: Modeling complex grouping relations in database design

entries.title: Hyperplane
entries.content: A subspace of one dimension less than its ambient space.
entries.example: A flat 2D plane dividing a 3D space

entries.title: Hypotenuse
entries.content: The longest side of a right-angled triangle, opposite the right angle.
entries.example: Hypotenuse of a triangle with legs 3 and 4 is 5

entries.title: Hypothesis Testing
entries.content: A statistical method that uses sample data to evaluate claims about a population parameter.
entries.example: Testing if a new drug works better than a placebo

entries.title: Hypothesis Testing Alpha
entries.content: The significance level, the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is actually true.
entries.example: Using alpha = 0.05 for a statistical significance test

entries.title: Hypothesis Testing Beta
entries.content: The probability of failing to reject the null hypothesis when it is actually false.
entries.example: Calculating the statistical power of a clinical trial

entries.title: Hypothesis Testing Null
entries.content: The default assumption that there is no effect, no difference, or no relationship in the population.
entries.example: Null hypothesis: the new teaching method has no effect on scores

entries.title: Hypothesis Testing Power
entries.content: The probability that a test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis.
entries.example: Ensuring sample size is large enough to achieve 80% power

entries.title: Harmonic Analysis
entries.content: A branch of mathematics concerned with the representation of signals or functions as the superposition of basic waves.
entries.example: Analyzing vibration frequencies in engineering

entries.title: Hilbert Transform
entries.content: An integral transform that shifts the phase of the spectral components of a signal by 90 degrees.
entries.example: Creating analytic signals in telecommunication
